prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: homepage hero banner frame for a mobile post-apocalyptic vehicle defense game
Primary request: Create a premium wide horizontal hero banner frame for a game start screen, designed to look professional, cinematic, and game-ready.
Scene/backdrop: fully transparent outside the banner silhouette
Subject: one centered wide command-banner panel, front-facing, no perspective
Style: post-apocalyptic sci-fi military UI, rugged bronze steel, dark graphite core, controlled amber light accents, subtle tactical polish, premium but not overly bulky
Composition: a wide horizontal banner with elegant armored top and bottom rails, restrained side braces, a calm dark center for title text, and subtle embedded tech detailing that does not clutter the reading area
Technical requirements: intended final size around 560x170; the center text area must remain large and quiet; avoid oversized frame thickness; keep the banner visually lighter and more refined than a heavy loot card; no text, no logos, no characters, no scenery, no detached wings, no large glowing blobs
Output goal: a clean professional title banner that can sit over a start screen background and immediately elevate the perceived production value

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: mission selection card background for a mobile game homepage
Primary request: Create a premium wide tactical mission card for stage selection on a game start screen, designed to look more professional and information-rich than a plain text block.
Scene/backdrop: fully transparent outside the card silhouette
Subject: one centered wide horizontal stage-selection card, front-facing, no perspective
Style: refined wasteland-tech metal UI, dark charcoal and weathered bronze, subtle amber and cool blue indicator lights, tactical panel feel, polished game-ready render
Composition: the card should have a broad central information area, a smaller top eyebrow zone, a dedicated lower strip where reward badges can sit, and subtle left/right side docking zones that visually support navigation arrows without making them mandatory
Technical requirements: intended final size around 560x200; keep the frame thinner and cleaner than a popup panel; preserve a strong readable central area for stage number and stage name; leave room near the lower portion for small chips or badges; no text, no icons, no arrows, no characters, no scenery, no giant decorative horns, no clutter
Output goal: a professional mission briefing card background that helps stage info feel structured and premium on the homepage
